title: pyrus malus subsp. acerba
intro: Pyrus malus subsp. acerba, also known as the crab apple, is a subspecies of the common apple tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and prevent the growth of certain bacteria. The health benefits of Pyrus malus subsp. acerba include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and even help to manage diabetes. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to treat a range of health issues.
